heading: Initial payments outside the United States
label: 8136 Initial payments outside the United States
text: If an employee is injured outside the continental United States, the Secretary of Labor may arrange and provide for initial payment of compensation and initial furnishing of other benefits under this subchapter by an employee or agent of the United States designated by the Secretary for that purpose in the locality in which the employee was employed or the injury occurred.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large (2d sentence of 4th par.)5 U.S.C. 793(2d sentence), .July 29, 1942, ch. 53356 Stat. 725The word “continental” is added on authority of the last sentence of the fifth paragraph of former section 793, which is carried into section 8137.Administration of this subchapter was transferred to the Secretary of Labor by section 1 of 1950 Reorg. Plan. No. 19, (see section 8145).64 Stat. 1271Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.
